claim: [CLAIM-V0] Four venue profiles (LMCS, JFR, MCS, TCS) built with HEADER + POLICIES + EDITORIAL + TURNAROUND + FORMALIZATION + AUTHOR-FIT sections each; every concrete field is either retrieved verbatim from a fetched venue homepage / ISSN-portal record / dblp record during 2026-05-07 14:30-14:55 JST or marked UNKNOWN with the specific information gap.

[CLAIM-V1] LMCS venue identifiers verified at fire time: eISSN 1860-5974 (online-only); homepage https://lmcs.episciences.org/ resolved live; DOI prefix 10.46298 (Episciences shared); EiC Stefan Milius; Executive Editors Brigitte Pientka + Fabio Zanasi; covered by Scopus + DBLP + WoS + MathSciNet + zbMATH per homepage footer; CC-BY 4.0; diamond OA with zero APC.

[CLAIM-V2] JFR venue identifiers verified at fire time: ISSN 1972-5787 (online-only); homepage https://jfr.unibo.it/ resolved live; AlmaDL OJS/PKP platform; CC-BY 3.0; diamond OA with zero APC; editorial board includes Avigad, Coquand, Geuvers, Gonthier, Harrison, Leroy, Wiedijk per editorial-team page; latest issue Vol. 13 No. 1 (2020), published 2020-12-21, per issue-archive page (5+ year publication gap surfaced).

[CLAIM-V3] MCS venue identifiers verified at fire time: print ISSN 1661-8270 (ISSN-L) + online ISSN 1661-8289 per portal.issn.org; homepage https://link.springer.com/journal/11786 (Springer-side auth-gated at fire time, marked UNKNOWN for direct content); dblp tracks Volumes 1 (2007/2008) through 20 (2026); Birkhauser/Springer Nature imprint; Switzerland-country per ISSN registry; DOI prefix 10.1007 (Springer-wide).

[CLAIM-V4] TCS venue identifiers verified at fire time: print ISSN 0304-3975 + online ISSN 1879-2294; homepage https://www.sciencedirect.com/journal/theoretical-computer-science resolved live; submission portal https://submit.elsevier.com/TCS exposed on homepage; APC USD 2,840 quoted at fire time; EiCs Lila Kari + Seth Pettie + Vladimiro Sassone per editorial-board page; DOI prefix 10.1016 (Elsevier-wide); CiteScore 2.5 + Impact Factor 1.0 published on homepage.

[CLAIM-S0] Scope-fit matrix 4 venues x 7 factors = 28 cells: 11 STRONG + 6 MODERATE + 1 WEAK + 10 UNKNOWN. Row totals JFR 4S/0M/0W/3U; LMCS 4S/0M/0W/3U; MCS 1S/3M/1W/2U; TCS 2S/3M/0W/2U. Every STRONG/MODERATE/WEAK score is anchored to a specific line or fetched-content quote in the corresponding venue profile per HALT_079_FAKE_SCORE discipline.

[CLAIM-C0] Four cover-letter framing drafts produced (LMCS + JFR + MCS + TCS), each ~225-230 words; title verbatim across all four (matches submission_log.txt L152 + L174 + tunnell_afm_R2.tex L74-77); abstract opening 2 sentences verbatim across all four split into 2 separate > -prefixed blockquote spans (sentence 1 = 21 words; sentence 2 = 35 words; both under 50-word ceiling per HALT_079_QUOTE_LENGTH); author + ORCID + GitHub + Zenodo DOI constants identical across all four; multi-submission disclosure paragraph identical across all four. Venue-specific elements vary only in salutation + journal name + section-routing line + per-venue framing paragraph.

[CLAIM-X0] Cross-venue compatibility note covers 4 sub-checks: D.1 parallel-submission policy (single-channel baseline across all four; no conflicts); D.2 AFM-clearance carry-forward (Item 25 L205 verbatim quote anchors clearance); D.3 reuse risk (Zenodo 10.5281/zenodo.19834824 + GitHub github.com/papanokechi/tunnell-cnp-lean4 prior-deposit compatibility; LMCS + JFR explicit-permit + MCS + TCS UNKNOWN exact 2026 wording); D.4 cover-letter consistency one-source-of-truth check returns CLEAN.

[CLAIM-D0] W21 LANE-1 decision packet emits 7-option synth menu (PICK_LMCS + PICK_JFR + PICK_MCS + PICK_TCS + PICK_OTHER + DEFER + WITHDRAW_PERMANENTLY) with per-option triggers and synth-discipline reminders. The 4 PICK_<vid> options are listed alphabetically (JFR / LMCS / MCS / TCS) within the menu table to avoid implicit ranking. No recommended-pick line appears in the packet per HALT_079_VENUE_SELECTION_OVERREACH discipline.

[CLAIM-L0] Item-splice spec for tex/submitted/submission_log.txt drafted contingent on synth pick: target Item number 26 (next sequential after Item 25; HALT_079_NUMBERING_OVERREACH discipline holds), 7 placeholder fields (FILENAME / STATUS / DATE / SUBMISSION_ID / HANDLING_EDITOR / VERDICT / COVERLETTER_SHA), 4 candidate venue-fill templates (LMCS / JFR / MCS / TCS), 3 non-templated synth options (PICK_OTHER + DEFER + WITHDRAW_PERMANENTLY). Spec is NOT applied at this T2 stage; apply-time discipline (re-compute live SHA + line-number) named.
